meanings.definition: lose (s.th.) or lose the right to (s.th.) by some error, offense, or crime
examples: you've forfeited your right to name your successor

meanings.definition: give up with the intent of never claiming again
examples: She gave up her children to her ex-husband when she moved to Tahiti
We gave the drowning victim up for dead

meanings.definition: give up in the face of defeat of lacking hope; admit defeat
examples: In the second round, the challenger gave up

meanings.definition: put an end to a state or an activity
give up what is not strictly needed
examples: he asked if they could spare one of their horses to speed his journey

meanings.definition: part with a possession or right
examples: I am relinquishing my bedroom to the long-term house guest
resign a claim to the throne

meanings.definition: leave (a job, post, or position) voluntarily
examples: She vacated the position when she got pregnant
The chairman resigned when he was found to have misappropriated funds
The squatters had to surrender the building after the police moved in

meanings.definition: give up or agree to forgo to the power or possession of another
examples: The last Taleban fighters finally surrendered

meanings.definition: stop maintaining or insisting on; of ideas or claims
examples: He abandoned the thought of asking for her hand in marriage
Both sides have to give up some claims in these negotiations

meanings.definition: allow the other (baseball) team to score
